--- SOURCE 1: About NDB - New Development Bank ---
URL: https://www.ndb.int/about-ndb/

SUMMARY:
The New Development Bank (NDB) is a multilateral development bank established by Brazil, Russia, India, China and South Africa (BRICS) with the purpose of mobilising resources for infrastructure and sustainable development projects in emerging markets and developing countries (EMDCs) .

FULL CONTENT:
# About NDB

## The Premier Bank for Emerging Economies

The New Development Bank (NDB) is a multilateral development bank established by Brazil, Russia, India, China and South Africa (BRICS) with the purpose of mobilising resources for infrastructure and sustainable development projects in emerging markets and developing countries (EMDCs).

Our strong mandate and potential put us in a unique position to contribute to global growth and development.

## Overview

Working hand in hand with our member countries and stakeholders, we leverage capital for development purposes to accelerate economic growth, as well as to achieve environmental and social sustainability to improve the lives of people in our member countries.

NDB strives to act as a catalyst in bridging the gap between the availability of financial resources and the growing needs of our founding members and EMDCs.

We play an active role in expanding our impact on EMDCs by providing financing for infrastructure and sustainable development projects.

At NDB, sustainability is at the core of our mandate. We seek to ensure that all projects financed by the Bank are implemented in a sustainable way and their ESG impacts are assessed, minimised and mitigated during project implementation.

## Our Strategy

Our General Strategy for 2022–2026 sets the course for our evolution into a leading provider of solutions for infrastructure and sustainable development for EMDCs.

## Building a Sustainable Future

NDB is dedicated to developing solutions to create a sustainable future for the world where we live. We integrate the principles of sustainability and sound management in everything we do.

## Our Focus Areas

Within the broad spectrum of infrastructure and sustainable development, NDB’s operation will focus on a range of key areas.

![Icon-Clean-Energy](https://www.ndb.int/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/Icon-Clean-Energy-62x62.png)
![Icon-Transport-Infrastructure](https://www.ndb.int/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/Icon-Transport-Infrastructure-62x62.png)
![Icon-Water-Sanitation](https://www.ndb.int/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/Icon-Water-Sanitation-62x62.png)
![Icon-Digital-Infrastructure](https://www.ndb.int/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/Icon-Digital-Infrastructure-62x62.png)
![Icon-Environmental-Protection](https://www.ndb.int/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/Icon-Environmental-Protection-62x62.png)
![Icon-Social-Infrastructure](https://www.ndb.int/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/Icon-Social-Infrastructure-62x62.png)

## History

Established in 2015, NDB has built a trusted institutional profile, underpinned by its diversified portfolio of infrastructure and sustainable development projects and strong international credit ratings.

--- SOURCE 3: New Development Bank: Home ---
URL: https://www.ndb.int/

SUMMARY:
Established in 2015 by BRICS countries, the New Development Bank is a multilateral development bank aimed at mobilising resources for infrastructure and sustainable development projects in BRICS and other EMDCs. NDB supports infrastructure and sustainable development projects in alignment with the development objectives of our members and their commitments under the SDGs and the Paris Agreement. NDB Board of Directors Approved City Bank Sustainable Infrastructure Project The New Development Bank finances projects and innovates tailored solutions to help build a more inclusive, resilient, and sustainable future for the planet. Expected development results are presented for selected projects financed by NDB in collaboration with partners, irrespective of the proportion of the Bank’s financing in the total project cost. Our Focus Areas
---------------

The New Development Bank finances projects and innovates tailored solutions to help build a more inclusive, resilient, and sustainable future for the planet.

[Focus Areas](https://www.ndb.int/about-ndb/focus-areas/)

![Image 3: Icon-Clean-Energy](https://www.ndb.int/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/Icon-Clean-Energy-62x62.png)

Clean energy and energy efficiency 

![Image 4: Icon-Transport-Infrastructure](https://www.ndb.int/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/Icon-Transport-Infrastructure-62x62.png)

Transport infrastructure

Social infrastructure

![Image 8: Icon-Digital-Infrastructure](https://www.ndb.int/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/Icon-Digital-Infrastructure-62x62.png)

Digital infrastructure

At a Glance
-----------

At NDB, we are dedicated to delivering sustainable, long-term returns. NDB’s funding strategy aims to ensure that sufficient resources are available to meet the Bank’s liquidity requirements while optimising borrowing costs. Our funding activities spread across the markets, currencies, formats and maturities so as to support the Bank’s project portfolio and financing needs.

Total amount of financing approved (USD)

39.0 billion

120 Projects

2,400 MW

14.7 million tonnes/year

35,000

288,800 m³/day

Amount of approvals has been translated into USD using exchange rates as of December 31, 2024.

Expected development results are presented for selected projects financed by NDB in collaboration with partners, irrespective of the proportion of the Bank’s financing in the total project cost. The numbers are rounded, and are based on the information available at the time of approval

Investor Relations
------------------

At NDB, we are dedicated to delivering sustainable, long-term returns. NDB’s funding strategy aims to ensure that sufficient resources are available to meet the Bank’s liquidity requirements while optimising borrowing costs. Our funding activities are spread across multiple markets, currencies, formats and maturities so as to support the Bank’s project portfolio and financing needs.

--- SOURCE 4: New Development Bank - Brics ---
URL: https://brics.br/en/about-the-brics/new-development-bank

SUMMARY:
The NDB is a multilateral development bank (MDB) created to mobilize resources to finance infrastructure and sustainable development projects

FULL CONTENT:
# New Development Bank

The NDB is a multilateral development bank (MDB) created to mobilize resources to finance infrastructure and sustainable development projects in developing countries.

## Origin and establishment

During the VI BRICS Summit, held in Fortaleza in 2014, a new cycle in the history of the group was inaugurated with the establishment of the New Development Bank (NDB) and the Contingent Reserve Arrangement.

The NDB is a multilateral development bank established with the goal of mobilizing resources to finance infrastructure and sustainable development projects in developing countries.

The decision to constitute the NDB was based on a viability study led by the national ministries of finance, which identified: (i) the need for financing in developing countries not covered by traditional multilateral financial institutions such as the World Bank; and (ii) the reduction in international credit capacity for development purposes.

The authorized capital of the NDB is 100 billion dollars. The paid-in capital is 52.7 billion dollars. There are 96 projects approved to date, with a total financing of 32.8 billion dollars.

## NDB governance

The NDB is based in Shanghai and has offices in São Paulo and Gujarat, India.

It is administered by the Board of Governors and composed of its members’ ministries of finance.

The bank has a rotating presidency that is periodically occupied by a representative from a BRICS country, while the other countries are responsible for nominating the four vice presidents.

The NDB has been presided over since 2023 by Dilma Rousseff, whose mandate will end in July 2025. The decision to extend the mandate is currently under discussion.

## NDB members

In addition to Brasil, Russia, India, China, and South Africa — the founding members of the NDB — the bank's membership expanded between 2021 and 2023 to include Bangladesh, the United Arab Emirates, and Egypt. Uruguay and Algeria are currently in the process of joining.

The new BRICS members are expected to join the NDB, a process that will follow the terms and procedures stated in the bank’s constitutive agreement.

Adhesion to the BRICS does not guarantee membership in the NDB.

## NDB projects in Brasil

In the context of the NDB, Brasil has an active portfolio of approximately USD 5 billion in sustainable infrastructure projects, emergency income support programs, sustainable mobility, climate change adaptation, basic sanitation, and renewable energy.

For more information, access: <https://www.ndb.int/>

## Contingent Reserve Arrangement (CRA)

The Contingent Reserve Arrangement (CRA) is a mutual financial support platform to which BRICS members can turn in the event of difficulties in their balance of payments.

The CRA is fully operational and can be accessed at any time by initiative of one of the members.

Through the CRA, members commit to provide international reserves in the amount of up to USD 100 billion, thus distributed: China (USD 41 billion), Brasil (USD 18 Bi), India (USD 18 Bi), Russia (USD 18 Bi) and South Africa (USD 5 Bi).

BRICS new members can request adhesion to the CRA, which will be analyzed by the agency’s Governing Board, in line with the Treaty for the Establishment of the BRICS Contingent Reserve Arrangement, dated July 15, 2014. In Brasil, the CRA was implemented through Decree No. 8,702/2016 [[1]](/en/about-the-brics/new-development-bank#_msocom_1) (Decreto n. 8,702/2016).

Up until this moment, no participant country has needed to request CRA resources.

![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/af/Flag_of_South_Africa.svg/40px-Flag_of_South_Africa.svg.png)

![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fe/Flag_of_Uruguay.svg/40px-Flag_of_Uruguay.svg.png)
![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/84/Flag_of_Uzbekistan.svg/40px-Flag_of_Uzbekistan.svg.png)
![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/9f/Flag_of_Indonesia.svg/40px-Flag_of_Indonesia.svg.png)
![Edit this at Wikidata](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/8/8a/OOjs_UI_icon_edit-ltr-progressive.svg/20px-OOjs_UI_icon_edit-ltr-progressive.svg.png)

The **New Development Bank** (**NDB**), formerly referred to as the **BRICS Development Bank**, is a [multilateral development bank](/wiki/Multilateral_development_bank "Multilateral development bank") established by the [BRICS](/wiki/BRICS "BRICS") nations (Brazil, Russia, India, China, and South Africa).[[1]](#cite_note-UIA-1) According to the agreement, "the bank shall support public or private projects through [loans](/wiki/Loan "Loan"), guarantees, equity participation and other [financial instruments](/wiki/Financial_instrument "Financial instrument")" and "shall cooperate with [international organizations](/wiki/International_organization "International organization") and other financial entities, and provide technical assistance for projects to be supported by the bank."[[2]](#cite_note-Agreement-2)

The initial [authorized capital](/wiki/Authorized_capital "Authorized capital") of the bank was US$100 billion divided into one million [shares](/wiki/Share_(finance) "Share (finance)") having a par value of US$100,000 each. The initial subscribed capital of the bank was US$50 billion divided into US$10 billion in paid-in shares and US$40 billion in callable shares. It was equally distributed among the five founding members–Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa. The agreement on the NDB specifies that each founding member will have one vote and that no member would have any veto powers.[[2]](#cite_note-Agreement-2)

The bank is headquartered in [Shanghai](/wiki/Shanghai "Shanghai"), China.[[3]](#cite_note-BRICS_Bank-3) The first regional office of the bank was opened in [Johannesburg](/wiki/Johannesburg "Johannesburg"), South Africa in 2016.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Subsequently, regional offices were established in [São Paulo](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Paulo "São Paulo") in Brazil, [Ahmedabad](/wiki/Ahmedabad "Ahmedabad") in India and [Moscow](/wiki/Moscow "Moscow") in Russia.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## History

The idea for setting up the bank was proposed by India at the [4th BRICS summit](/wiki/4th_BRICS_summit "4th BRICS summit") in 2012 held in [New Delhi](/wiki/New_Delhi "New Delhi").[[6]](#cite_note-6) As the establishment of a development bank would help in simplifying mutual settlement and lending operations amongst the BRICS nations, reducing dependence on the [US dollar](/wiki/US_dollar "US dollar") and the [Euro](/wiki/Euro "Euro"), the leaders of these nations agreed to set up anew [multilateral development bank](/wiki/Multilateral_development_bank "Multilateral development bank") at the [5th BRICS summit](/wiki/5th_BRICS_summit "5th BRICS summit") held in [Durban](/wiki/Durban "Durban"), South Africa on 27 March 2013.[[7]](#cite_note-VOA-7) On 15 July 2014, on the first day of the [6th BRICS summit](/wiki/6th_BRICS_summit "6th BRICS summit") held in [Fortaleza](/wiki/Fortaleza "Fortaleza"), Brazil, the five member states signed the agreement on the New Development Bank, which made provisions for the legal basis of the bank.[[2]](#cite_note-Agreement-2) In a separate agreement, a [reserve currency pool](/wiki/Foreign-exchange_reserves "Foreign-exchange reserves") worth US$100 billion was set up by the members.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/02/The_President%2C_Development_Bank_of_BRICS_Countries%2C_Shri_K.V._Kamath_calls_on_the_Prime_Minister%2C_Shri_Narendra_Modi%2C_in_New_Delhi_on_May_28%2C_2015.jpg/250px-The_President%2C_Development_Bank_of_BRICS_Countries%2C_Shri_K.V._Kamath_calls_on_the_Prime_Minister%2C_Shri_Narendra_Modi%2C_in_New_Delhi_on_May_28%2C_2015.jpg)

On 11 May 2015, [K. V. Kamath](/wiki/K._V._Kamath "K. V. Kamath") was appointed as the first president of the bank.[[9]](#cite_note-9) According to the bank, the policies and procedures for all functional areas were approved at the board of directors meeting in January 2016.[[10]](#cite_note-10) On 27 February 2016, the NDB signed an agreement with the Government of China and local government of Shanghai to establish the headquarters of the bank in [Shanghai](/wiki/Shanghai "Shanghai").[[11]](#cite_note-11) On 19 July 2016, the NDB reported that it successfully issued the bank's first [green financial bond](/wiki/Climate_bond "Climate bond") with issue size of [RMB](/wiki/RMB "RMB") 3 billion, with a tenure of five years in the China interbank bond market.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

![Timeline showing NDB creation milestones](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a8/NDB_Creation_Milestones.png/250px-NDB_Creation_Milestones.png)

On 20 July 2016, the first annual meeting of the NDB board of governors was held in Shanghai. The participants of the meeting discussed the bank's future work and development and gave a positive assessment to the bank's work. At the meeting, the first green financial bond issuance in [renminbi](/wiki/Renminbi "Renminbi") was highlighted as a milestone event for the NDB.[[13]](#cite_note-13) In 2016, the NDB board of directors approved the first set of projects in all members of the bank.[[14]](#cite_note-14)[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16) On 21 December 2016, the NDB signed its first loan agreement.[[17]](#cite_note-17) The NDB received AA+ credit ratings from S&P Global Ratings ([S&P](/wiki/Standard_%26_Poor%27s "Standard & Poor's")) and Fitch Ratings ([Fitch](/wiki/Fitch_Ratings "Fitch Ratings")) in August 2018, which enables the bank to offer full suite of financial products to its public and private sector clients.[[18]](#cite_note-18)[[19]](#cite_note-19) In April 2020, the New Development Bank established an Emergency Assistance Facility that sought to finance costs related to the fight against the Coronavirus and soften the blow from the economic impact. In addition to committing US$5 billion to this cause, the NDB targeted to provide up to 10 billion dollars.[[20]](#cite_note-20)

In March 2022, the NDB announced that "sound banking principles" required that all new transactions with Russia be put on hold.[[21]](#cite_note-21) Even though the NDB quickly cut ties with Russia, Fitch Ratings still downgraded the NDB from stable to negative on its Long-Term Issuer Default scale.[[22]](#cite_note-22)[[23]](#cite_note-l320-23) In May 2022, the New Development Bank set up a regional office in India in the state of Gujarat with the goal of financing and observing infrastructure projects in both India and Bangladesh.[[24]](#cite_note-24) In May 2023, Saudi Arabia expressed its intention to join the NDB.[[25]](#cite_note-25)

## Structure and Objectives

### Corporate Governance

According to the Articles of Agreement, the main organs of the bank are:

Information about the composition of the NDB board of governors is available on the bank's official website.[[26]](#cite_note-26)

The NDB president is elected on a rotational basis from one of the founding members, and there are four vice presidents from each of the other four founding members.[[27]](#cite_note-27)

[K. V. Kamath](/wiki/K._V._Kamath "K. V. Kamath"), from India, is the first elected president of the NDB. He was replaced as president by [Marcos Prado Troyjo](/wiki/Marcos_Prado_Troyjo "Marcos Prado Troyjo") from Brazil since 7 July 2020. Marcos Troyjo was elected president of the New Development Bank on 27 May 2020.[[28]](#cite_note-28) Following [Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva](/wiki/Luiz_In%C3%A1cio_Lula_da_Silva "Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva") election, the Brazilian government solicited his renouncement, after then he was replaced by [Dilma Rousseff](/wiki/Dilma_Rousseff "Dilma Rousseff") by 24 March 2023.[[29]](#cite_note-29)

### Capital

The New Development Bank has an initial subscribed capital of US$50 billion and an initial authorized capital of US$100 billion. The initial subscribed capital is equally distributed among the founding members. The payment of the amount initially subscribed by each founding member to the paid-in capital stock of the bank will be made in dollars in 7 installments.[[2]](#cite_note-Agreement-2) Each member cannot increase its share of capital without all other four members agreeing. The bank will allow new members to join but the BRICS capital share cannot fall below 55%.[[30]](#cite_note-ready-30)

### Objectives

The bank aims to contribute to the development plans established nationally through projects that are socially, environmentally and economically sustainable. Taking this into account, the main objectives of the NDB can be summarized as follows

## Membership

NDB Member Countries[[31]](#cite_note-31)

![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/0/05/Flag_of_Brazil.svg/40px-Flag_of_Brazil.svg.png)
![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/f/f3/Flag_of_Russia.svg/40px-Flag_of_Russia.svg.png)
![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/4/41/Flag_of_India.svg/40px-Flag_of_India.svg.png)
![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fa/Flag_of_the_People%27s_Republic_of_China.svg/40px-Flag_of_the_People%27s_Republic_of_China.svg.png)
![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/af/Flag_of_South_Africa.svg/40px-Flag_of_South_Africa.svg.png)
![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f9/Flag_of_Bangladesh.svg/40px-Flag_of_Bangladesh.svg.png)
![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/cb/Flag_of_the_United_Arab_Emirates.svg/40px-Flag_of_the_United_Arab_Emirates.svg.png)
![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fe/Flag_of_Uruguay.svg/40px-Flag_of_Uruguay.svg.png)
![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fe/Flag_of_Egypt.svg/40px-Flag_of_Egypt.svg.png)
![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/77/Flag_of_Algeria.svg/40px-Flag_of_Algeria.svg.png)
![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/21/Flag_of_Colombia.svg/40px-Flag_of_Colombia.svg.png)
![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/84/Flag_of_Uzbekistan.svg/40px-Flag_of_Uzbekistan.svg.png)

The Agreement on the New Development Bank entered into force in July 2015, with the official declaration of all five states that have signed it. The five founding members of the Bank include Brazil, Russia, India, China and South Africa.[[32]](#cite_note-s634-32)

The NDB's Articles of Agreement specify that all [members of the United Nations](/wiki/Member_states_of_the_United_Nations "Member states of the United Nations") could be members of the bank, however the share of the original BRICS group can never be less than 55% of total voting power. The current voting power of the original five members is more than 90%.

In 2016, some experts considered that expanding the NDB's membership to be crucial to its long-term development by helping boost the bank's business growth.[[33]](#cite_note-scmp.com-33)

According to the Bank's General Strategy: 2017–2021, the NDB plans to expand membership gradually so as not to overly strain its operational and decision-making capacity.[[34]](#cite_note-Strategy_-_New_Development_Bank-34)

In September 2021, Bangladesh, the United Arab Emirates and Uruguay joined the NDB.[[35]](#cite_note-35)

In December 2021, the NDB admitted Egypt as a new member.[[36]](#cite_note-36) [Algeria](/wiki/Algeria "Algeria") formally presented a request to join after the Algerian president visit to China in 2023, and was admitted as a member in 2024.[[37]](#cite_note-x652-37) Algeria officially has been given membership status of the BRICS New Development Bank (NDB) in September 2024.[[38]](#cite_note-b009-38)

Potential or prospective members include [Honduras](/wiki/Honduras "Honduras") and [Zimbabwe](/wiki/Zimbabwe "Zimbabwe").[[39]](#cite_note-39)[[40]](#cite_note-40)

## Shareholding structure

According to Articles of Agreement of the New Development Bank, the initial authorized capital of the bank is divided into 1 million shares, having a par value of $100,000. Each founding member of the bank has initially subscribed 100,000 shares, in a total of $10 billion, of which 20,000 shares correspond to paid-in capital, in a total of $2 billion and 80,000 shares correspond to callable capital, in a total of $8 billion.[[2]](#cite_note-Agreement-2)

![Pie chart showing equal distribution of shares between the NDB shareholders](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4b/NDB-Chart-BRICS.png/250px-NDB-Chart-BRICS.png)

The current distribution of shares between NDB member countries is presented in the following table.

## Activities

### Projects

According to the Bank's General Strategy, sustainable infrastructure development is at the core of NDB's operational strategy in 2017–2021, and the Bank will dedicate about two-thirds of financing commitments in its first five years to this area.[[34]](#cite_note-Strategy_-_New_Development_Bank-34)

The New Development Bank is planning to give a priority to projects aimed at developing [renewable energy](/wiki/Renewable_energy "Renewable energy") sources. As it was stated by the bank, it wants to [cooperate](/wiki/Cooperation "Cooperation") with other institutions in accelerating ‘green’ financing expansion and promoting [environment protection](/wiki/Environmental_protection "Environmental protection").[[41]](#cite_note-:1-41)

![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0a/Breakdown_of_NDB%27s_loan_approvals_by_sector.png/250px-Breakdown_of_NDB%27s_loan_approvals_by_sector.png)

The NDB has expressed interest in funding projects that conform to high environmental standards, including those in the field of infrastructure, such as energy, railways and highways in the future.[[42]](#cite_note-42) At the same time, according to [K. V. Kamath](/wiki/K._V._Kamath "K. V. Kamath"), the NDB President, one of the key strategies of the bank will be financing profitable projects (bankable) with return on capital.[[43]](#cite_note-Russia_&_India_Report-43) The NDB wants "to fund projects that are creative and bring benefits to the local people and environment", said Vice President Zhu Xian.[[44]](#cite_note-auto-44)

The leadership of the member countries task the bank with developing a strong pipeline of projects and responding in a fast and flexible manner to aspirations and interests of its members.[[45]](#cite_note-45)

As of 6 March 2019, the NDB Board of Directors approved 30 projects with loans aggregating over approx. USD 8 billion.[[46]](#cite_note-46)

The bank has begun non-sovereign operations with loans approved in Brazil, South Africa, and Russia. In May 2024, BRICS Development Bank announces to allocate US$1.115 bi to [Rio Grande do Sul](/wiki/Rio_Grande_do_Sul "Rio Grande do Sul") for rebuilding the urban and rural infrastructure after the [2024 floods](/wiki/2024_Rio_Grande_do_Sul_floods "2024 Rio Grande do Sul floods").[[47]](#cite_note-h610-47)

### Bonds

In March 2016, the NDB announced that it will do a [bond](/wiki/Bond_(finance) "Bond (finance)") issue in China to raise funding domestically on the Chinese market. The bond issue is likely to happen in the second quarter of the year 2016, said the NDB Vice President Leslie Maasdorp. He added the bank is starting to finalize the exact size of this bond issue.[[41]](#cite_note-:1-41)

On 18 July 2016 the NDB successfully issued its first green financial bond with issue size of RMB 3 billion, tenor of 5 years in China onshore interbank bond market. The bond's nominal interest rate is 3.07%. The bank became the first international financial institution that issued a green financial bond in the China onshore bond market. The proceeds of the bond will be used for infrastructure and sustainable development projects in the member countries.[[48]](#cite_note-48)

In February 2019, the bank successfully placed CNY 3 billion RMB-denominated bond in the China Interbank Bond Market, and it was priced at the lower end of announced pricing range.[[49]](#cite_note-49)

On May 18, 2022, the New Development Bank issued a 7 billion RMB - 3 year bond into the Chinese Interbank Bond Market (CIMB), making it the largest bond ever made by foreign issuers. It received an Issuer Rating(IR) of AA+ from both S&P and Fitch.[[50]](#cite_note-50)[[51]](#cite_note-51)

## Relations with other institutions

### Approach

The Articles of Agreement of the bank say that the NDB was established to complement the existing efforts of multilateral and regional financial institutions for global growth and development. Moreover, the NDB is authorized by its founders to cooperate within its mandate with other international organizations, as well as national entities (public or private), in particular with international financial institutions and national development banks.[[2]](#cite_note-Agreement-2)

The NDB President K.V.Kamath stresses that the bank regards other international financial institutions, including IMF and World Bank as partners, not rivals.[[52]](#cite_note-52)

### Asian Development Bank

In July 2016, the NDB signed with [Asian Development Bank](/wiki/Asian_Development_Bank "Asian Development Bank") (ADB) a memorandum of understanding on strategic cooperation. The two institutions expressed willingness to work together through co-financing and knowledge exchanges in areas including sustainable development projects in renewable energy, energy efficiency, clean transportation, sustainable water management, and sewage treatment.[[53]](#cite_note-53)

### Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank

According to a representative of the Bank's management, the NDB and AIIB are sister institutions in many respects. These two banks have complementary mandates and different geographic focuses, with the NDB being more focused on BRICS countries. At the same time, there is a certain overlapping between mandates of the NDB and the AIIB, as both of them are aimed at developing infrastructure and pay a special attention to sustainable development. However, due to the fact that current financing and investment patterns are inadequate in meeting investment needs, there is "space for newcomers", he said.[[54]](#cite_note-:2-54)

In February 2016, the president of the NDB dismissed concerns over overlapping of interests of China-backed AIIB and the NDB.[[55]](#cite_note-:0-55)

### World Bank

According to media reports, other multilateral development institutions, including the [World Bank Group](/wiki/World_Bank_Group "World Bank Group") (WBG), have expressed an intention to work together with the NDB.[[56]](#cite_note-56) In September 2016, NDB and World Bank Group signed a memorandum of understanding on cooperation and it was announced that the NDB and WBG's cooperative efforts will focus primarily on infrastructure.[[57]](#cite_note-57)

## Reception

![Bank's logo](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/e/e2/New_Development_Bank_Logo.svg/250px-New_Development_Bank_Logo.svg.png)

### Prominent scholars

In July 2014, [Nobel Prize](/wiki/Nobel_Prize "Nobel Prize") winning economist [Joseph Stiglitz](/wiki/Joseph_Stiglitz "Joseph Stiglitz") said the NDB marks a "fundamental change in global economic and political [power](/wiki/Power_(social_and_political) "Power (social and political)")." In his opinion, "The existing institutions just don't have enough [resources](/wiki/Resource "Resource")."[[58]](#cite_note-58)

In the analysis of academic [Suisheng Zhao](/wiki/Suisheng_Zhao "Suisheng Zhao"), China's role in the creation of the NBD is "a symbolic gesture to create a sort of IMF clone writ small toward reshaping the Western-dominated international financial architecture."[[59]](#cite_note-59)

### Officials

Chinese Premier [Li Keqiang](/wiki/Li_Keqiang "Li Keqiang") called the opening of the NDB an "important step forward" in cooperation among BRICS countries. "This is great progress in financial cooperation among developing countries and emerging economies, as well as a helpful supplement to the [global financial system](/wiki/Global_financial_system "Global financial system")," Li Keqiang said at a meeting with K. V. Kamath in Beijing in July, 2015.[[60]](#cite_note-60)

China Finance Minister [Lou Jiwei](/wiki/Lou_Jiwei "Lou Jiwei") said that the establishment of the NDB and the Beijing-led AIIB would encourage existing multilateral institutions to do better. At a [news conference](/wiki/News_conference "News conference") at the conclusion of the [G20](/wiki/G-20_major_economies "G-20 major economies") meeting of finance ministers and central bank governors in Shanghai he added that the New Development Bank could help drive aggregate global [demand](/wiki/Demand "Demand").[[61]](#cite_note-61)

### Bankers

8 July 2015, [VTB](/wiki/VTB "VTB") [CEO](/wiki/Chief_executive_officer "Chief executive officer") [Andrey Kostin](/wiki/Andrey_Kostin "Andrey Kostin") said that the NDB is "important because it's a first institution created by the BRICS countries." "Actually we are talking about the [institutionalization](/wiki/Institutionalisation "Institutionalisation") of the BRICS process, and that's quite important I think. It's a permanent working instrument which will be working every day and which will be in the heart of BRICS cooperation," he added.

According to former [Reserve Bank of India](/wiki/Reserve_Bank_of_India "Reserve Bank of India") governor [Raghuram Rajan](/wiki/Raghuram_Rajan "Raghuram Rajan"), the NDB "Is a co-operative effort between all BRICS countries." "We have already reached contingency reserve agreement (CRA). This is second. Let's see how it develops. Lots of hopes embedded in it for greater cooperation (among the) BRICS countries," he told reporters in February 2016.[[62]](#cite_note-62)

## Logo and identity

![Bank's logo and BRICS flags](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fa/NDB-HQ-LOGO.jpg/250px-NDB-HQ-LOGO.jpg)

The logo of the New Development Bank borrows from the mathematical concept of a [Mobius strip](/wiki/M%C3%B6bius_strip "Möbius strip") that symbolizes the idea of continuous transformation. Its nature is not to disrupt but drive change in the existing system from within.[[63]](#cite_note-63)[[64]](#cite_note-64)

The logo consists of a triangle in motion at one end signifying balanced evolution. The other end, moving in the opposite direction, is a [propeller](/wiki/Propeller "Propeller") that represents speed and [dynamism](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/dynamism "wikt:dynamism"). These two entities are held together by a wireframe, the skeletal basic of infrastructure. The logo is rendered in gradient of green that symbolizes [sustainability](/wiki/Sustainability "Sustainability"). This constant motion symbolizes the values that the bank strives to live by – [agility](/wiki/Agility "Agility"), [innovation](/wiki/Innovation "Innovation") and [continuous transformation](/wiki/Transformation_(function) "Transformation (function)").[[65]](#cite_note-65)

enriched_points.expanded_content: Established in 2015 by BRICS nations, the NDB aims to mobilize resources for sustainable development and infrastructure projects in emerging markets and developing countries.
Unlike traditional banks like the World Bank, the NDB focuses on underserved regions, providing flexible financing tailored to the specific needs of member countries.
The NDB enhances global governance by promoting cooperation among emerging economies, reducing reliance on Western-dominated financial systems, and addressing development gaps in infrastructure.
The NDB encounters challenges such as geopolitical tensions among member states, balancing diverse economic interests, and maintaining financial sustainability amid global economic fluctuations.
With expanding membership and a focus on sustainable projects, the NDB has significant potential to influence global development strategies and foster economic resilience in its member countries.
